title: 10 best herbal creams for stomach pain

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with stomach pain because they contain curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that soothes digestive issues. When applied topically to the skin, curcumin reduces inflammation and relaxes muscles in the abdominal area, providing relief from cramps, bloating, and discomfort associated with irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), gastritis, and other stomach conditions. Additionally, turmeric's natural antibacterial properties help combat infections that can exacerbate stomach pain, promoting a healthy gut environment.
remedy_properties: Curcuminoids: Curcuminoids, particularly curcumin, have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of stomach pain such as bloating, cramps, and diarrhea.

Cineole: Cineole, a terpene found in turmeric, has natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help relieve abdominal pain and discomfort by reducing muscle contractions and inflammation in the digestive tract.

Tumerone: Tumerone, another terpene in turmeric, has antimicrobial properties that can help reduce the growth of harmful bacteria in the gut, such as Helicobacter pylori, which can contribute to stomach pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that helps reduce inflammation and alleviate stomach pain.
Roots: Containing a high concentration of turmerone, a volatile oil that soothes digestive issues and relieves abdominal discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Grate 50 grams of fresh turmeric rhizome and mix with 10 grams of beeswax in a small saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 15 minutes or until the beeswax is fully melted and combined.
Add 20 milliliters of sweet almond oil and 5 milliliters of coconut oil to the mixture and stir well.
Remove from heat and let cool to room temperature before whipping with a hand mixer for 10 minutes.
Pour the curd-like cream into an airtight container and store in the refrigerator for up to two weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with stomach pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the digestive tract. The active compound, gingerol, in ginger creams reduces inflammation and relaxes the muscles in the stomach, providing quick relief from cramps, nausea, and bloating. Additionally, ginger's warming sensation stimulates digestion and improves blood circulation to the stomach area, helping to alleviate discomfort and pain associated with indigestion and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).
remedy_properties: Inflammation reduction: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, alleviating discomfort and pain associated with conditions like indigestion, bloating, and gas.

Digestive enzyme stimulation: Herbal ginger creams contain digestive enzymes that stimulate the production of gastric juices, improving digestion and absorption of nutrients, which can help relieve symptoms of nausea, vomiting, and stomach cramps.

Antioxidant activity: Ginger's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals in the body, reducing oxidative stress and damage to the stomach lining, which can contribute to inflammation and pain.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of ginger have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate stomach pain and discomfort, making them a popular choice for medicinal creams.
Barks: Ginger barks contain compounds like shogaol and gingerols, which have natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects that can provide relief from stomach pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of fresh ginger rhizome zingiber officinale and wash it thoroughly under cold running water.
Grind the ginger into a fine paste using a mortar and pestle for approximately 10 minutes.
Mix 25 milliliters of sweet almond oil with 5 grams of beeswax and heat the mixture in a double boiler until the wax melts.
Add the ginger paste to the oil mixture and stir well for about 2-3 minutes before removing from heat.
Pour the cream into sterilized glass jars and store it in the refrigerator at a temperature between 40°f to 45°f.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with stomach pain because it targets the muscles in the stomach, known as the smooth muscle, which contract and relax causing cramps and spasms. The menthol present in peppermint creams numbs these muscles, relaxing them and reducing the pain caused by irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and other digestive disorders. Additionally, peppermint's natural anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach lining, further alleviating discomfort and pain.
remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol's analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties help to numb and soothe the stomach, reducing pain and discomfort caused by digestive issues such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).

Catechins: The flavonoids present in peppermint creams, particularly catechins, have been shown to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the digestive tract, alleviating cramps and spasms that can cause stomach pain.

Eucalyptol: Eucalyptol's ability to stimulate digestion and relieve gas and bloating helps to ease discomfort and alleviate symptoms of indigestion, making it a valuable component in herbal peppermint creams for stomach pain relief.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in menthol and other bioactive compounds, peppermint roots are used to create topical creams that provide fast-acting relief from stomach pain.
Rhyzomes: The underground stems of peppermint plants contain high concentrations of menthone and limonene, which help to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from your garden or store-bought ones for a total of 20 grams.
Dry the peppermint leaves in a warm oven at 40°c for 24 hours to preserve their potency.
Combine the dried peppermint leaves with 100g of coconut oil and 50g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture gently over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax has melted and the mixture is smooth.
Pour the cooled cream into sterilized jars and store them in the refrigerator for up to three months or freeze for longer storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow creams helps with stomach pain because they provide a soothing, protective barrier for the mucous membranes in the digestive tract. The gel-like substance coats and calms irritated areas, reducing inflammation and discomfort. Herbal marshmallow creams also contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help to ease cramps and spasms, allowing the digestive system to function more smoothly. This natural relief can bring prompt and lasting comfort to those suffering from stomach pain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Marshmallow root contains mucilages, which are thick, protective substances that soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with stomach pain.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal marshmallow creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and polyphenols, which help reduce swelling and alleviate symptoms of stomach pain, such as cramps and bloating.

Glycosides: Marshmallow root also contains glycosides, which have been shown to relax the muscles in the digestive tract and stimulate the production of mucus, further soothing and protecting the stomach lining from irritation and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the marshmallow plant are used to make medicinal creams for stomach pain because they contain mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and calms irritated digestive tissues.
Barks: The barks of the marshmallow plant are used to create topical creams and ointments that can be applied directly to the skin to relieve stomach cramps and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried marshmallow root (althaea officinalis) from a trusted source.
Grind the 100g of dried marshmallow root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Combine 20ml of sweet almond oil and 10ml of beeswax in a double boiler setup over low heat.
Add 20g of the ground marshmallow root to the oil mixture and stir for 5 minutes as it melts.
Remove from heat and let cool before transferring to sterilized glass jars for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with stomach pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe digestive issues such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and gastritis. The creams' active ingredient, glycyrrhizin, has been shown to reduce inflammation in the gut, calming irritated tissues and reducing symptoms like bloating, cramps, and discomfort. Additionally, herbal licorice creams can help relax the stomach muscles, further alleviating pain and discomfort associated with digestive issues.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Helps to reduce inflammation and soothe digestive issues by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, which can cause stomach pain and discomfort.

Licoricidin and Licorisoflavan A: Possess anti-ulcer properties, protecting the mucous lining of the stomach and preventing damage from acid reflux and stomach acids, thereby reducing stomach pain and inflammation.
Flavonoids (Licoisoflavones): Act as a natural antispasmodic, relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract and reducing muscle spasms that can contribute to stomach cramps and pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and alleviating stomach pain.
Rhyzomes: Contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach, providing relief from discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered root with 20g of beeswax, 30g of coconut oil, and 50g of shea butter in a double boiler to melt the ingredients.
Allow the mixture to heat for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it reaches a temperature of 120°f to 140°f (49°c to 60°c).
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool slightly before whipping it with an electric mixer for 5-7 minutes until it becomes light and fluffy.
Pour the cream into sterile jars or tubes, label them clearly, and store in a cool place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with stomach pain because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe digestive issues. The plant's root and leaves have been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of indigestion, bloating, and cramps. The cream's topical application allows for direct absorption into the skin, where it can quickly address discomfort in the abdominal area. Additionally, dandelion's bitter properties stimulate digestion and promote gut health, making it a natural remedy for alleviating stomach pain and promoting overall digestive well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Dandelion cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like taraxasterol, which helps reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), such as stomach cramps and bloating.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in dandelion cream, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to conditions like gastritis and peptic ulcers.

Soothing astringent effects: Dandelion's natural astringency helps reduce inflammation and soothe irritated mucous membranes in the digestive tract, providing relief from heartburn, acid reflux, and stomach discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in inulin, a prebiotic fiber that soothes and calms digestive issues, making it an effective ingredient in creams for stomach pain relief.
Buds: Containing bitter compounds like taraxasterol, buds have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including stomach pain, by reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry taraxacum officinale leaves in shade or using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 24 hours.
Grind dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix ground taraxacum officinale with 200 grams of coconut oil, 100 grams of beeswax, and 20 drops of peppermint essential oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at low temperature for 30 minutes until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture is smooth and consistent.
Remove the cream from heat and let it cool to room temperature before pouring into sterilized containers and storing in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with stomach pain because they contain essential oils that stimulate digestion, reduce inflammation, and relax the muscles in the digestive tract. The anethole compound present in fennel oil has a natural antispasmodic effect, which soothes cramps and discomfort caused by irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), acid reflux, and other gastrointestinal issues. Additionally, fennel's calming properties help to ease bloating, gas, and indigestion, providing fast relief from stomach pain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Fennel cream's antispasmodic properties help to relax the smooth muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps associated with stomach pain.

Carminative properties: Fennel cream's carminative properties can help relieve gas and bloating by reducing the formation of gas bubbles in the digestive tract, which can alleviate stomach pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots are rich in volatile oils, which help soothe digestive issues and alleviate stomach pain.
Seeds: Fennel seeds have carminative properties, making them effective in reducing flatulence and bloating associated with stomach discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and clean foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers in large quantities and wash thoroughly with lukewarm water.
Combine one part of the fresh plant material with two parts of olive oil and steep for 30 minutes under gentle heat.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to separate the plant extract from the oil, squeezing gently to release excess liquid.
Mix 20 grams of beeswax with 100 milliliters of the plant-infused oil and heat slowly in a double boiler until melted and fully incorporated.
Allow the cream to cool and solidify completely before transferring it to an airtight container for storage at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with stomach pain because of its unique combination of botanical ingredients that work together to soothe and calm the digestive system. The creams contain extracts of maidenhair fern, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the gut, alleviating symptoms such as cramping and bloating. Barks: Containing a unique combination of tannins and phenolic acids, which help to reduce inflammation and protect the mucous membranes in the stomach.
remedy_recipe: Grind 100 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the powdered ginkgo biloba with 50 grams of sweet almond oil in a double boiler and heat gently for 20 minutes.
Add 5 grams of beeswax to the mixture and stir until fully incorporated, then remove from heat and let cool for 30 minutes.
Stir the mixture every 10 minutes as it cools and waits 1 hour to allow the wax to solidify completely into a creamy consistency.
Transfer the cooled cream to an airtight container and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months, using within 3 days after opening.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with stomach pain because its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties help to calm digestive issues. The apigenin in chamomile binds to GABA receptors, promoting relaxation of the muscles in the stomach and intestines, which can ease cramps, spasms, and discomfort associated with IBS, acid reflux, and other digestive disorders. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers at peak bloom for maximum potency and essential oils content.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm dark place or using a food dehydrator on low heat for 24 hours.
Grind dried chamomile flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 30 minutes.
Combine 20 grams of powdered matricaria chamomilla with 100 milliliters of melted coconut oil in a double boiler and stir gently for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove solids and store the cream in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm creams helps with stomach pain because of its unique ability to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the digestive tract. The cream's active ingredient, mucilages, forms a protective barrier that coats and calms inflamed areas, reducing irritation and discomfort. remedy_recipe: Gather dried ulmus rubra bark and other required ingredients including sweet almond oil and beeswax within an hour.
Combine one cup of dried ulmus rubra bark with two cups of boiling water and let steep for 30 minutes within 15 minutes.
Strain the liquid mixture using a cheesecloth or coffee filter and discard the solids within 10 minutes.
Mix four tablespoons of sweet almond oil and four tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler and heat until melted within 20 minutes.
Combine the herbal infusion with the oil and wax mixture and stir well before pouring into containers for storage within 15 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Barberry creams helps with stomach pain because they contain berberine, a natural compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh berberis vulgaris roots and wash them thoroughly under running water.
Dry the roots in a warm oven at 150°c for 3 hours or until they reach a brittle state.
Crush dried berberis vulgaris roots into fine powder using a mortar and pestle grinder.
Mix 50g of powdered berberis vulgaris with 100ml of coconut oil and 20ml of beeswax in a double boiler for 2 hours or until fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into glass jars, label, and store at room temperature for 2 weeks before use allowing it to mature and thicken.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh red clover flowers measuring approximately 100g to ensure optimal potency.
Combine the harvested flowers with 500ml of cold-pressed coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 4 weeks, shaking the jar daily, allowing for infusion.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding solids and reserving 250ml of liquid extract.
Mix the liquid extract with 50g of beeswax and 20g of coconut oil in a double boiler at low heat, stirring occasionally, until the mixture reaches a smooth consistency.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of serenoa repens and rinse them thoroughly under cold running water.
Dry the harvested leaves in a well-ventilated area for at least two days or use a dehydrator on low heat.
Grind 20 grams of dried serenoa repens leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground herb with 100 grams of cold-pressed coconut oil and 50 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at low temperature for about 30 minutes or until it reaches a smooth, creamy consistency.

remedy_recipe: Gather dried lavandula angustifolia flowers, coconut oil, beeswax, and vitamin e oil in a clean workspace.
Combine 20 grams of dried lavender flowers with 100 milliliters of carrier oil in a double boiler setup.
Heat the mixture for 30 minutes while gently stirring until the mixture reaches a temperature of 65 degrees celsius.
Allow the infused oil to cool and then whip it with 50 grams of melted beeswax and 5 milliliters of vitamin e oil.
Pour the cream into sterilized containers and store them in a cool dark place for at least 24 hours before using.

remedy_recipe: Sterilize equipment and work area by autoclaving for 20 minutes.
Combine 10 grams of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves and 100 milliliters of boiling water to steep for 15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a heat-resistant measuring cup.
Add 200 grams of petroleum-based cream and blend until fully incorporated with a hand blender for 5 minutes.

Combine the dried herb with 20 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil in a clean glass jar.

Mix 10g of beeswax with 30g of coconut oil and heat until melted in a double boiler setup.
Combine the herbal infusion with the melted wax-oil mixture and stir well, then pour into sterilized jars for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow creams helps with stomach pain because it contains active compounds that work to soothe and calm the digestive system. The anti-inflammatory properties of yarrow help reduce swelling and irritation in the stomach, while its carminative properties aid in eliminating gas and bloating. Additionally, yarrow's antispasmodic effects ease muscle cramps and spasms that can contribute to stomach pain. By addressing these underlying issues, yarrow creams provide fast and effective relief from digestive discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce swelling and discomfort in the digestive tract, alleviating stomach pain and cramps.
Antispasmodic: Yarrow's antispasmodic property relaxes the muscles in the digestive system, reducing spasms and cramping that can cause stomach pain and discomfort.
Digestive stimulant: Yarrow's digestive stimulant property helps to increase digestive enzymes and improve digestion, which can help alleviate symptoms of indigestion, bloating, and stomach pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used to create a calming and anti-inflammatory effect, which helps to soothe stomach discomfort.
Flowers: The flowers contain flavonoids and terpenes that have antispasmodic properties, helping to reduce stomach cramps and spasms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried acherle leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated weighing approximately one ounce.
Combine harvested material with carrier oils such as coconut or olive oil at a ratio of 1 part plant material to 4 parts oil.
Steep the mixture for three hours in a warm dark place allowing the plant compounds to infuse into the oil.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container discarding solids and reserving liquid.
Blend reserved liquid with beeswax and essential oils as needed and whip until creamy applying immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with stomach pain because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm the digestive tract. The natural antioxidants in these creams also reduce inflammation, which can help alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and other digestive disorders. Additionally, pot marigold creams may help to repair the gut lining, reducing discomfort and pain associated with stomach issues. This natural remedy offers a gentle and effective way to find relief from stomach ache.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold contains flavonoids and carotenoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of stomach pain such as cramps, bloating, and diarrhea.

Antioxidant: The antioxidants present in pot marigold creams protect the digestive system from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, helping to reduce the risk of chronic conditions like irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) that can cause stomach pain.

Gastroprotective: Pot marigold's active compounds have been shown to have gastroprotective effects, helping to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, which can help alleviate symptoms of stomach pain such as heartburn, acid reflux, and gastritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help soothe digestive issues, making them a valuable ingredient in medicinal creams.
Barks: Contain anti-inflammatory properties that can alleviate stomach pain and discomfort when applied topically as a cream.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried calendula flowers measuring one ounce by weight and grind into a fine powder using mortar and pestle.
Mix the calendula powder with one tablespoon of coconut oil and three tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler until melted and fully incorporated.
Add three tablespoons of distilled water to the mixture and stir well before heating for 10 minutes at a low temperature.
Remove from heat and let cool slightly before whipping with an electric mixer for five minutes or until creamy and smooth.
Transfer the cream to sterilized containers filling them to the top and sealing tightly for storage in the refrigerator.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for stomach pain are topical treatments infused with natural herbs and plant extracts that provide relief from digestive discomforts. These creams work by reducing inflammation, soothing irritated tissues, and relaxing muscles in the abdominal area. For example, peppermint oil cream can help alleviate IBS symptoms, while ginger-based creams can ease nausea and cramps. By providing fast and gentle relief, herbal creams improve quality of life for those suffering from stomach pain, allowing them to manage their condition with confidence and resume daily activities without discomfort.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat stomach pain with herbal creams is to apply topical remedies that soothe and calm the digestive system. Ginger-based creams are particularly effective in reducing inflammation and discomfort. Look for creams containing ginger essential oil or powder, and apply a thin layer to the affected area 2-3 times a day as needed. Additionally, peppermint oil creams can help alleviate cramps and spasms. Always follow instructions and patch test before using any new herbal remedy.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to stomach pain that are treated with herbal creams are conditions such as eczema, acne, and psoriasis. These topical treatments often feature herbal ingredients like aloe vera, tea tree oil, and chamomile, which soothe and calm irritated skin. The creams may also contain anti-inflammatory properties to reduce redness and swelling, providing relief from discomfort and itching associated with these conditions.
